subject: LU-6427 osd: race between destroy and load LMV

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 3:

unfortunately, we can not clear LOHA_EXIST flag in osd_object_destroy
[12:12:19 PM] wangdi: it seems mdd_unlink (or close) still trying to do a lot things after dt_object_destroy, until the transaction stop
[12:12:30 PM] wangdi: see mdd_close and mdd_unlink
[12:13:41 PM] wangdi: the alternative way might be clear the EXIST flag before release ldlm lock

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

this is very tricky approach, IMO. logically OSD should return an error if someone is trying to access non-existing object. in turn, the upper layers should recognize such a case properly.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

I don't think it's ever safe to clear this flag. And even if it could be made safe, I don't think that clearing it is a good way to solve any problem.

Patch Set 4:

John, could you explain better please. we can handle that totally internally to OSD, meaning no exposure for LOHA_EXISTS at all - then the users would need to call some method (like dt_attr_get()) to get -ENOENT, but that's probably too radical at the moment.

Patch Set 4:

Alex, I begin to think resolve this in OSD might be too hard at the moment. It seems the current implementation still keep accessing the object even after the object is destroyed, see mdd_unlink (mdd_close). that saying touching EXIST flag might need much more work than I thought.

Patch Set 4:

probably the very first question should be why the original thread doing dt_attr_get() against destroyed object doesn't panic. then osd_destroy()/osd_attr_get() can handle that using an internal to OSD flag. logically the two cases (dt_attr_get() on a not-yet-created object and destroyed object) should produce the same result, IMO -- ENOENT.

Patch Set 5:

Why isn't this being fixed in osd_index_it_init()?

Patch Set 5:

Well, it is not safe to access the object, once it has been destroyed, i.e. dt_object_destroy() is called.

And Alex also thought LOHA_EXIST is overused somehow.

Patch Set 5:

John, LOHA_EXISTS being clear doesn't really change much as it doesn't control object's existence, it only "caches" that state. a private flag in OSD which is used only by OSD is fine, IMHO. personally I'd like to get rid of LOHA_EXISTS at all, but that's a big project..

Patch Set 5:

also, could you tell what specifcially wront with the semantics for LU_OBJECT_HEARD_BANSHEE, LOHA_EXISTS and DEAD_OBJ ? I don't think they even overlap.

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

It was easy to reproduce the original ZFS issue by looping stat and lfs mkdir -i1 -c2 + rmdir.


As Alex has pointed out the thread still holds a reference to the object via tsi_corpus. So if the object is dying then the second find will block.

Even if the tsi_corpus reference is removed this is still prone to deadlock.

Patch Set 6:

"As Alex has pointed out the thread still holds a reference to the object via tsi_corpus. So if the object is dying then the second find will block."

Hmm, In the patch, this refcount will be removed before it try another find. 

Ah, then we should add a test case to verify this.

Btw: Alex, will you proceed the fix inhttp://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/14423/ , if you do, I will drop this one. Please let me know, thanks.

Patch Set 6:

>> "As Alex has pointed out the thread still holds a reference to the object via tsi_corpus. So if the object is dying then the second find will block."

> Hmm, In the patch, this refcount will be removed before it try another find. 

No, that's the mti_object reference. tsi_corpus has its own reference.

Di - It looks like this patch is causing sanityn test 37 to fail. Would you please look into this? More information can be found at:
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/94d3ac22-0028-11e5-ac6b-5254006e85c2
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/5b123bc0-078a-11e5-bad2-5254006e85c2

https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/40bd17f6-fc3e-11e4-a4dd-5254006e85c2

Patch Set 12:

iirc, there are cases when MDD accesses just destroyed objects to fetch attributes. if we change OSD to return an error, then MDD should either avoid such a call or handle -ENOENT properly?

Patch Set 12:

"iirc, there are cases when MDD accesses just destroyed objects to fetch attributes. if we change OSD to return an error, then MDD should either avoid such a call or handle -ENOENT properly?"

Not sure I understand correctly. Do you mean mdd_xattr_get? hmm if OSD can return error in this case, then MDD will return the error. Do I miss sth?

Patch Set 12:

"Di - It looks like this patch is causing sanityn test 37 to fail. "

James: this is testing ZFS with DNE configuration. you saw the failure on test 37 only for this patch, because other patch never run ZFS dne configuration. i.e. the failure  is not related with this patch. Anyway I will look at the failure.

parents.subject: LU-6502 lnet: remove unnecessary NULL check

commit.subject: LU-6427 osd: race between destroy and load LMV
commit.message:
LU-6427 osd: race between destroy and load LMV

Do not get LMVEA if the object has been destroyed,
because it needs to iterate the master object, which
is dangerous for ZFS OSD.

Add oo_destroyed flags to avoid accessing destroyed
directory.

Add sanityn.sh 83 to verify accessing destroyed striped
directory will not cause panic.
